name: brainiac
description: Read-only search over your Brainiac vault for the Claude Desktop Chat tab. Requires the `brain` engine already installed on this machine (npx brainiac-install) — this bundle never installs or vendors the engine itself, it only spawns it.
long_description:
Brainiac is a local, any-LLM second brain (plain Markdown + YAML). This extension is a thin Node.js stdio shim: it locates your HOST-installed `brain-mcp` engine (the same one `pip install brainiac-cli[mcp]` / `uv tool install brainiac-cli[mcp]` puts on PATH) and spawns it, piping stdio through unchanged. It exposes exactly the same read-only verb set and classification egress gate as the CLI's `brain search`/`get`/`recent`/`dossier`/`bases-query` — no write, draft, or host-broker verbs. If the engine isn't installed yet, the extension fails to start with an actionable error: install it with `npx brainiac-install`, then reopen this extension.

Do not also run `brain connect --client claude-desktop` for the same vault — pick ONE registration path (this extension, or the CLI-managed claude_desktop_config.json stanza), never both. `brain doctor` flags it if both are present.

tools.name: search
tools.description: Hybrid BM25+dense retrieval over the vault, egress-filtered.

tools.name: get
tools.description: Fetch one full note by id, egress-filtered.

tools.name: recent
tools.description: List the most recently created/updated notes, egress-filtered.

tools.name: dossier
tools.description: One-call decision-state sweep: decision layer + sources, separated, egress-filtered.

vault.title: Vault path
vault.description: Path to your Brainiac vault workspace (the folder containing vault/brain, vault/raw). Required: Claude Desktop has no reliable working directory to fall back to when this extension spawns the engine, and there is no such thing as a registry of a 'default vault' — leaving this blank would silently point at an empty phantom vault, so this bundle requires an explicit path.

max_tier.title: Max egress tier
max_tier.description: Classification ceiling for this LLM-facing session: Public, Internal, Confidential, Restricted, or MNPI. Defaults conservatively to Internal; raise it only for an explicitly approved server. This bundle never widens the ceiling the engine itself enforces.
